title: Kanna Nee Thoongadaa Lyrics Baahubali 2

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kanna Nee Thoongadaa?

The song is a soothing lullaby that conveys a sense of calmness and devotion.

Who composed the music for Baahubali 2?

The music for Baahubali 2 was composed by MM Keeravani.

When was Kanna Nee Thoongadaa released?

The song was released in 2017.

What is the context of the song in the movie?

The song is used as a lullaby, adding a layer of emotional depth to the film's narrative.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the epic saga of Baahubali 2, Kanna Nee Thoongadaa serves as a soothing lullaby that brings a sense of peace and tranquility. Composed by MM Keeravani, the song is a gentle melody that evokes feelings of devotion and calmness. It is used in the film to enhance the emotional depth of the narrative, providing a moment of respite amidst the grandeur. The song's soothing tones and heartfelt lyrics make it a perfect choice for relaxation and introspection.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the serene and soothing vibes of Kanna Nee Thoongadaa from the epic film Baahubali 2. Composed by the legendary MM Keeravani, this lullaby beautifully captures the essence of tranquility and devotion. Dive into a world of calmness and let the melody guide you to peace.
stanzaMeanings: Is it right, is it flowing, is it happy?
Is it right for the eyes to close over flowers? The girl's heart is melting every day like the moon. Please sleep a little.
Kanna, please sleep, my dear Kanna, please sleep.
Carrying the beauty of your smile is enough. Kanna, please sleep, my dear Kanna, please sleep.
The music from your lips is enough. Kanna, please sleep, my dear Kanna, please sleep.
Kanna, please sleep, my dear Kanna, please sleep.
You stole clothes while the girls were bathing, playful one, you never rest.
You stole butter from the pot every day, pure one, please sleep.
Enough, Kanna, your mischief has surrounded the sky with darkness. Rest your head and close your eyes.
Kanna, please sleep, my dear Kanna, please sleep.
In the middle of the garden, I wandered, I roamed, I was lost near you.
In the middle of the forest, I was dizzy and lay down, lost in your beauty.
You playfully tease me. In the morning, you tease yourself too. You have taken my pain with your words.
Kanna, please sleep, my dear Kanna, please sleep.
Is it right, is it flowing, is it happy?
Madhana, Madhusudhana, beautiful one, enchanting one.
Kanna, is it right, is it flowing, is it happy? Kanna, Kanna.
Krishna, come, come, dear Kanna, please sleep.
